definitions: The set of chemical reactions that take place in chloroplasts during photosynthesis, reducing carbon dioxide and other compounds into glucose.
A metabolic pathway found in the stroma of chloroplasts in photosynthetic organisms, which uses energy from ATP and NADPH to fix carbon dioxide and produce sugars.
examples: The Calvin-Benson cycle is crucial for plant growth.
Understanding the Calvin-Benson cycle helps explain carbon fixation.
ATP and NADPH are consumed during the Calvin-Benson cycle.
